company_context.claim: OpenAI adopted Linear across thousands of users as teams scaled and needed alignment across complex dependencies.
Ramp uses Linear as a core engineering workflow while scaling a high-velocity engineering, product, and design organization.

pains.claim: Disconnected team tools and local workflows can make cross-team collaboration difficult as organizations scale.
Legacy tools and slow processes made it harder for Retool teams to balance product work with business-critical maintenance.

triggers.claim: Scaling from small teams to thousands of employees created a need for systems that reduce friction and preserve speed.
Ramp's constant stream of product feedback became hard to manage as the company grew.

objections.claim: Enterprise evaluation may involve SAML, SCIM, granular admin controls, invoice or PO billing, and onboarding support.
Workspace admins or owners are required to configure the Slack integration for the workspace.

current_alternatives.claim: OpenAI teams previously used different tools and systems across teams, creating islands of process.
Retool's story names legacy tools and slow processes as the before state.

persona_anchors.persona_name: Product and design leaders coordinating roadmaps
persona_anchors.seniority: Head, VP, Product Manager, Design Leader

company_context.claim: Ramp's design and product organization uses Linear for roadmap visibility, spec lists, quarterly planning, and monthly check-ins.
Linear's projects feature is built for features or large units of work with planned outcomes and optional documents.

pains.claim: Product planning needs visibility into ownership, roadmap pieces, and context in one place.
Customer requests need to be linked to issues or projects so feedback is accessible to product teams.

triggers.claim: Quarterly planning and monthly check-ins can trigger evaluation of roadmap and project planning workflows.
Customer feedback from support tools, CRM tools, email, and Slack can trigger customer-request workflows in Linear.

objections.claim: Product leaders may need to verify that timeline views, projects, initiatives, and customer requests cover planning needs without overloading issue-level workflows.
Business plan features such as private teams, guests, Linear Insights, Linear Asks, and Zendesk or Intercom integrations affect package selection.

current_alternatives.claim: Ramp previously used separate software for Gantt charts before using Linear roadmap views.
Projects can attach resources, documents, links, milestones, and progress graphs, replacing scattered planning artifacts for project work.

evidence.claim: Small teams can initially coordinate work in their heads or through chat before scale makes friction visible.

awareness_stages.message_implication: Early-stage buyers may not name the category yet; evidence should start from ordinary coordination friction.

evidence.claim: OpenAI's story describes cross-team handoffs, stale statuses, and disconnected systems as collaboration problems.

awareness_stages.message_implication: Problem-aware buyers can be anchored on handoff failure, tool friction, and scaling coordination pain.

evidence.claim: Linear Docs groups product features across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, views, AI, integrations, and analytics.

awareness_stages.message_implication: Solution-aware buyers are likely evaluating issue tracking and product planning workflows as a connected system.

evidence.claim: The Linear customers page presents customer stories from OpenAI, Ramp, Brex, Scale, Mercury, Cash App, Retool, and others.

awareness_stages.message_implication: Product-aware buyers can use customer evidence to compare whether Linear fits teams like theirs.

evidence.claim: Linear's pricing page lists Free, Basic, Business, and Enterprise packaging, with Enterprise sold as custom annual billing.

awareness_stages.message_implication: Most-aware buyers are ready for package, admin, security, rollout, and procurement details.

search_intent.query_pattern: cross team issue tracking handoffs getting lost
product development system for teams and agents
search_intent.likely_persona: Product and design leaders coordinating roadmaps

search_intent.query_pattern: linear slack integration create issues from slack

search_intent.likely_persona: Product and design leaders coordinating roadmaps

search_intent.query_pattern: linear alternatives jira shortcut github issues
search_intent.likely_persona: Product and design leaders coordinating roadmaps

buying_committee_notes.claim: Enterprise package evaluation includes invoice or PO billing, SAML and SCIM, granular admin controls, security, onboarding, priority support, and account management.
Slack integration setup requires a workspace admin, or an owner on Enterprise, to configure the integration.
Customer Requests can be enabled by admins and then used by workspace members to add customer feedback to issues and projects.

exclusions.claim: Do not include linear algebra, equations, television, or regression results when researching Linear because the identity source sets those as negative keywords.
Do not treat broad non-software project management teams as the core ICP unless the source ties them to product development, engineering, customer feedback, or software delivery workflows.
